title: 10 best herbal capsules for chills
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for chills are a natural remedy designed to alleviate symptoms of chills, fever, and body aches. These capsules contain potent herbal extracts that work together to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and regulate body temperature. Examples of herbs used in these capsules include willow bark, ginger, and peppermint, which have been shown to provide relief from chill-related discomforts. By taking these capsules, individuals can experience a significant reduction in chills and associated symptoms, allowing them to resume their daily activities with ease and comfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with chills because they contain Echinacea, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to boost the immune system. The antiviral properties of Echinacea help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the body, which can cause chills. Additionally, Kansas coneflower capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to relieve fever and discomfort associated with chills. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals can help to maintain a healthy immune system and alleviate symptoms of chills and other cold and flu-related issues.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Echinacea contains compounds like cichoric acid and echinacein, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, reducing the severity of viral infections such as the common cold and flu, which can cause chills.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Kansas coneflower contains polyphenols and flavonoids that may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of chills, fever, and other symptoms associated with infection or illness.

Immunomodulatory properties: Echinacea has been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, such as neutrophils and macrophages, which play a crucial role in fighting off infections and reducing the severity of symptoms like chills.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, helping to boost the body's natural defenses against infections that can cause chills.

Polysaccharides: The polysaccharides present in Echinacea angustifolia may stimulate the immune system, enhancing its ability to fight off pathogens and reduce the severity of symptoms associated with chills.
Triterpene Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the underlying causes of chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used because they contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds, including alkylamides and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Roots: The roots are also rich in echinacoside, a glycoside that is thought to contribute to the plant's ability to reduce fever and alleviate chills.
Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia contain a significant amount of polysaccharides, which are believed to stimulate the immune system and enhance its ability to fight off infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots from a trusted supplier.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered root with 250 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of silica.
Use a capsule machine to fill 200 empty capsules with the gelatin mixture.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with chills because they possess a unique combination of herbs that work synergistically to promote sweating, thereby helping to eliminate toxins and reduce body temperature. The capsules contain Adiantum capillus-veneris, which has been traditionally used to treat fever and cold-related symptoms. Additionally, the presence of other herbs like ginger and licorice root helps to stimulate digestion and improve overall circulation, further contributing to the reduction of chills. By promoting a sense of warmth and comfort, maidenhair tree capsules provide relief from the discomfort caused by chills.
remedy_properties: Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Has natural anti-inflammatory and warming properties that can help to reduce chilliness and alleviate cold-like symptoms.
Echinacea (Echinacea spp.): Contains immunomodulatory compounds that can help to boost the immune system, reducing the severity of chills and other cold-related symptoms.
Peppermint (Mentha piperita): Has natural cooling properties that can help to reduce fever and alleviate chilliness, as well as ease digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Bilobalide helps with chills by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation, which in turn helps to warm the body and alleviate the sensation of coldness.
Quercetin: Quercetin helps with chills by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to reduce the severity of chills and other flu-like symptoms.

Ginkgolide B: Ginkgolide B helps with chills by preventing platelet-activating factor (PAF) from causing inflammation and blood clotting, which can contribute to the development of chills and other flu-like symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are responsible for the medicinal properties of Ginkgo biloba.
Buds: They are also used, as they contain higher amounts of flavonoids and terpenoids compared to other parts, which helps in alleviating chills.

Seeds: Although not as commonly used as leaves, Ginkgo biloba seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including chills, due to their unique bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind 200 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 20 grams of the ginkgo biloba powder with 80 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of water.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture and seal them using a capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with chills because they contain eucalyptus oil, which has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, it can help to alleviate congestion and reduce the severity of chills by relaxing bronchial muscles and easing mucus buildup in the airways. Additionally, the menthol content in Tasmanian blue gum capsules can help to cool the body down, providing relief from chills and feverish sensations.
remedy_properties: Decongestant properties: The eucalyptol present in Eucalyptus globulus essential oil may help relieve congestion and open airways, which could potentially alleviate chills caused by colds or flu.
Antimicrobial effects: Eucalyptus oil has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which might help combat the underlying infection causing the chills.
Inflammation reduction: The flavonoids present in Eucalyptus globulus may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with chills, such as fever, headache, and body aches.
remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (1,8-Cineole): This terpene has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, helping to relieve chills and reduce the severity of cold and flu symptoms.
Catechin: As a phenolic compound, catechin has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the underlying causes of chills, such as fever and infection.
Limonene: This terpene has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of chills by fighting off viral infections and soothing irritated tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of essential oils, particularly eucalyptol, which provides decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic compounds that help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of the common cold.
Stems: Some parts of the stem may also be used for their essential oil content, but it's less common than leaves and barks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted supplier within 2 years of processing.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 60°c for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 20 grams of gelatin in a large bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the eucalyptus globulus mixture using a small spoon within 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with chills because they contain the calming and soothing properties of lavender, which can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with chills. The herbal extract has a natural ability to relax muscles and calm the mind, making it an effective remedy for individuals who suffer from recurring or severe chills. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with chills.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce body temperature and ease discomfort caused by chills, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: The antiviral and antimicrobial properties in lavender capsules can help combat viral infections that often cause chills, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
Sedative and relaxing properties: Lavender's sedative and relaxing effects can help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety related to chills, and promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps with chills by acting as a natural antihistamine, reducing histamine release and alleviating symptoms associated with chills.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that helps with chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help calm the body and reduce chill symptoms.
Lavandulol: A terpene that helps with chills by displaying anxiolytic and sedative effects, which can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, alleviating chills.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are commonly used because of their calming and soothing effects, which can help alleviate chills and fever.

Stems: The stems of Lavandula angustifolia are also used, possibly due to their containing compounds that have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve chills and fever symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of lavandula angustifolia flowers at full bloom when the plant is in optimal condition.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 60°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 10 grams of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with 0.3 grams of the powdered flower each and seal the capsules.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with chills because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the body. The herb's antispasmodic properties help to ease muscle tension, reducing feelings of shivering and discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm has a gentle warming effect on the body, helping to dispel chilliness and promote overall relaxation. By addressing the root causes of chills, herbal lemon balm capsules provide natural relief from this uncomfortable symptom, allowing individuals to feel warm, calm, and comfortable once again.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Lemon balm's antiviral properties help combat viral infections that can cause chills, such as the common cold or flu.

Sedative and anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's sedative properties can help alleviate feverish symptoms of chills, while its anti-inflammatory effects may reduce body aches and pains associated with illness.
Antispasmodic activity: Lemon balm's ability to relax muscle spasms can help relieve coughs and congestion that often accompany chills, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound that helps alleviate chills by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's immune response.
Volatile oils (especially linalool and linalyl acetate): Terpenic compounds that help lower body temperature and reduce chills by acting on the hypothalamus and the body's thermoregulatory centers.
Melissic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce chills by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which in turn help to regulate body temperature and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for chills due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules for chills due to their high content of antioxidants and essential oils that help reduce inflammation and fever.
Roots: They are used to make capsules for chills due to their medicinal properties, including their ability to stimulate digestion and reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: Dry the melissa officinalis leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1 week to preserve their potency and flavor.
Crush 30g of the dried melissa leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered melissa leaves with 100g of gelatin to create a uniform blend for encapsulation.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the melissa leaf blend using a capsule filling machine or spoon.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with chills because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, ginger capsules can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as chills and fever. The warming effect of ginger also helps to increase circulation and promote sweating, which can help to break down and eliminate toxins from the body, further reducing chill symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate chill symptoms by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.
Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties of ginger help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and cough associated with chills.
Antioxidants: Ginger's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate chill symptoms like fever, sore throat, and body aches.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol is a phenolic compound that helps alleviate chills by reducing inflammation and relieving nausea, which can be caused by colds or flu.
Shogaol: Shogaol is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and warming properties, which helps to increase blood circulation and relieve chills.
Zingerone: Zingerone is a terpene that helps reduce inflammation and has a warming effect on the body, which can help alleviate chills and relieve cold-like symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which provide anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also widely used for their ability to relieve nausea and reduce fever, making them a popular ingredient in remedies for chills.
Buds: Buds of the plant are sometimes used for their ability to promote sweating and reduce body temperature, helping to alleviate chills and fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried ginger root by hand or with a mechanical harvester in the morning.
Dry the harvested root in a single layer at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours or until it reaches moisture content.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 200-300 milligrams of the powdered root with 100-200 milligrams of a binding agent like cellulose in a small bowl.
Fill 500-unit capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine or a manual filling device.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato capsules helps with chills because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and warm the body from within. The herbs used in these capsules have been traditionally used to treat fever, colds, and flu, and have been shown to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chills. By promoting blood circulation and releasing excess heat, wild potato capsules help to calm the body's natural response to infection or stress, providing relief from shivering and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antipyretic property: Wild Potato Capsules have been shown to reduce fever and relieve chills by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and lowering body temperature.
Anti-inflammatory property: The capsules' anti-inflammatory activity helps to alleviate chill symptoms such as fatigue, headache, and muscle pain by reducing inflammation and swelling in the body.

Antioxidant property: Wild Potato Capsules contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of chills and other symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterols: These triterpene saponins have anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing properties, which can help alleviate chills by decreasing the body's inflammatory response and reducing the production of heat-producing compounds.

Taraxasterol acetates: These triterpene saponins also exhibit anti-inflammatory and fever-reducing activities, which can contribute to a reduction in chills by suppressing the body's inflammatory response and regulating body temperature.

Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol, quercetin): These plant-based compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate chills by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, and promoting a healthy immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make capsules for chills because they contain active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Seeds: Used to make capsules for chills because they contain saponins that have fever-reducing and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for chills because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of taraxacum kok-saghyz root, dried for 2 weeks to remove excess moisture, and 50g of gelatin capsules.
Weigh and mix 20g of the dried taraxacum kok-saghyz root powder with 10g of cornstarch powder in a bowl.
Mix 10g of glycerin with 10g of water in a separate bowl for the capsule filling liquid.
Fill each gelatin capsule with 250mg of the taraxacum kok-saghyz root powder mixture, sealing them with a capsule filler.
Label and store the herbal taraxacum kok-saghyz capsules in a cool, dark place for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with chills because they contain a natural sedative compound called valerenic acid, which helps to relax the body's nervous system. When taken as a supplement, valerian can help to reduce feelings of anxiety and restlessness that can contribute to chills. Additionally, valerian has been shown to increase the production of heat in the body, helping to warm individuals who are feeling chilly. By promoting relaxation and increasing body heat, herbal valerian capsules can be an effective way to alleviate chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety, stress, and restlessness, making it easier for the body to warm up and regulate its temperature.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling and pain associated with chills, allowing the body to focus on recovering from an infection or other underlying cause of chills.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Valerian root has been shown to exhibit antiviral and antimicrobial activity, which helps to combat the underlying cause of chills, such as a viral or bacterial infection, by inhibiting the growth and replication of pathogens.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid helps with chills by reducing inflammation and modulating the nervous system's response to cold stress.
Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid helps with chills by acting as an antispasmodic and vasodilator, which helps to regulate body temperature and reduce muscle tension.
Valeranone: Valeranone helps with chills by exhibiting sedative and anxiolytic properties, which can help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to manage chills.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high content of valerenic acid, which is responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Roots: They are also rich in valepotriates and valeoptirates, which contribute to their cholinergic and sedative effects.
Roots: They have been traditionally used to reduce anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness, making them a popular choice for chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried valeriana officinalis roots from a reliable source to ensure quality and freshness.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Sift the powder through a 40 mesh sieve to achieve a uniform particle size and texture.
Mix 10g of the sifted powder with 50g of gelatin capsules in a small bowl and stir well for 2 minutes.
Fill the mixture into 100 gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper capsules helps with chills because they contain piperine, a bioactive compound that has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When consumed as a supplement, piperine can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to chills. Additionally, piperine's warming properties can help to increase circulation and warm the body from within, providing relief from chilliness. By addressing underlying inflammation and promoting warmth, herbal black pepper capsules can provide effective natural support for reducing chills.
remedy_properties: Piperine: Piperine, a bioactive compound found in black pepper, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms of chills by decreasing inflammation and swelling.

Antiviral and antimicrobial activities: The active compounds in black pepper capsules, including piperine and other phytochemicals, exhibit antiviral and antimicrobial activities that can help combat viral and bacterial infections that may cause chills.

Digestive warmth and circulation: Black pepper capsules can also stimulate digestion and improve blood circulation, which can help to generate body heat and reduce feelings of coldness and shivering associated with chills.
remedy_constituents: Piperine: Piperine, an alkaloid found in Piper nigrum, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills by modulating the body's thermoregulatory response.

Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene present in Piper nigrum, exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help alleviate chills and fever by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Vasodilatory phenolic acids: Piper nigrum contains various phenolic acids, such as ferulic and caffeic acids, that possess vasodilatory properties, helping to increase blood flow and reduce fever, thereby alleviating symptoms of chills.
remedy_parts: Seeds: These are used due to their high piperine content, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate chills and body aches.
Leaves: The leaves of Piper nigrum contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and chills.
Fruits: The fruits of Piper nigrum, also known as peppercorns, contain piperine and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and relieve chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest piper nigrum berries when they are ripe and mature to ensure optimal potency and flavor.
Clean the berries thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris and dry them immediately to prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried piper nigrum berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered piper nigrum with a binding agent such as gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powdered piper nigrum mixture and seal them tightly to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. Berberine, a key compound in goldenseal, has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort associated with chills. Additionally, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help combat viral infections that can cause chills. By taking goldenseal capsules regularly, you may find relief from cold and flu symptoms, including fever, headache, and body aches, allowing your body to recover more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound in Goldenseal, has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that help to combat infections that can cause chills, reducing their severity and duration.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid content in Goldenseal, including berberine and related compounds, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to alleviate symptoms such as fever, sweating, and general discomfort associated with chills.

Berbamine: Berbamine, another key compound in Goldenseal, has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the body's response to infection and reduce inflammation, making it an effective adjunctive treatment for managing chills.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: This alkaloid helps with chills by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing the severity of chills and fever.
Hydrastine: This alkaloid contributes to the antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects of Hydrastis canadensis, helping to alleviate chills and body aches.
Vasakine: Although less information is available about Vasakine, it's another alkaloid in Hydrastis canadensis that may help with chills by displaying antimicrobial properties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Hydrastis canadensis are the most commonly used part, as they contain high concentrations of berberine, a key active compound.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their similar composition to roots and are often harvested together.
Leaves: The leaves of Hydrastis canadensis are used, but they contain less berberine than the roots and rhyzomes, making them a secondary option.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried hydrastis canadensis roots in the fall season.
Clean and dry the roots in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 10-20 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the powder and seal them tightly.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with chills because they possess unique properties that promote thermogenesis, or heat generation, in the body. The active compounds in these capsules, such as cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin, have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to regulate body temperature and alleviate chill sensations. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been traditionally used to warm the body from within, making it a popular remedy for treating chills, colds, and flu-like symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals, which can contribute to the development of illnesses like chills by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Ceylon cinnamon capsules, such as cinnamaldehyde, can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with symptoms like chills, fever, and body aches.

Warming properties: Ceylon cinnamon has natural warming properties that can help increase blood flow and warm the body, which can be particularly helpful in reducing chills and promoting feelings of warmth and comfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, contains a high concentration of essential oils that help to warm the body and relieve chills.
Barks: The bark of the Ceylon cinnamon plant has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and alleviate chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum bark and dry it in shade for at least 48 hours to preserve its potency.
Grind 250 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered cinnamomum verum with 50 grams of silica gel as a filler agent in a ratio of 5:1 by weight to improve flowability.
Fill empty capsules with a mixture of 500 milligrams of powdered cinnamomum verum and 250 milligrams of silica gel, and seal the capsules immediately after filling.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate them for up to 1 year to maintain their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with chills because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and fever. The capsules' antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat infections that often cause chills, such as colds, flu, and sinusitis. Additionally, elder's adaptogenic effects help the body cope with stress and promote overall well-being, making it a natural remedy for alleviating chill symptoms. This holistic approach can lead to faster recovery and improved health.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effect: Elder capsules contain vasodilatory compounds like flavonoids, which help to expand blood vessels and increase blood flow, reducing the sensation of coldness and chills.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like anthocyanins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as fever and body aches associated with chills.
Antiviral activity: Elder capsules contain antiviral compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of viruses that can cause chills and flu-like symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have been traditionally used to combat fevers and reduce body temperature, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules for chills.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the elder plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with chills such as joint pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves of suitable quality for drying and processing.
Dry the plant material in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator at low temperature.
Crush the dried sambucus nigra into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered sambucus nigra with 500 milliliters of vodka for 2 weeks to create a tincture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 300 milligrams of the prepared tincture and store in an airtight container for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bloodroot capsules helps with chills because they contain sanguinarine, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, bloodroot can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a contributing factor to feelings of chilliness. Additionally, bloodroot's antioxidant properties help to boost the immune system, allowing the body to better fight off infections and illnesses that may be causing the chills in the first place. By addressing these underlying causes, bloodroot capsules can help to alleviate symptoms of chills and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Bloodroot contains saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and alleviate chills by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in bloodroot, such as sanguinarine and chelerythrine, have antimicrobial and antiviral effects that can help combat infections that often cause chills, such as the common cold or flu.

Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds found in bloodroot have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory properties, which means they can help regulate the immune response and reduce symptoms of infection-related chills, such as fever and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of bloodroot contain alkaloids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with chills.

Barks: Bloodroot bark contains triterpenoid saponins, which are known for their ability to stimulate the immune system and help regulate body temperature, making it a popular choice for medicinal capsules against chills.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried sanguinara canadensis root bark and gelatin capsules for a total of 250 milligrams per capsule.
Grind 100 milliliters of dried sanguinaria canadensis root bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix the powdered sanguinaria canadensis with 150 milliliters of pure water in a glass bowl and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove any solids and discard the residue.
Fill gelatin capsules with the liquid extract and dry them in a single layer on parchment paper for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce body temperature. The herb calms the respiratory system, soothing coughs and congestion that often accompany chills. Additionally, pot marigold's antiviral and antibacterial qualities help to combat infections that can cause chills. By promoting a healthy immune response, these capsules work to alleviate symptoms of chills and fever, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold contains apigenin, a flavonoid that reduces inflammation and fever, helping to alleviate chills caused by inflammatory conditions.
Antiviral property: The herb contains luteolin, which exhibits antiviral activity against various viruses, including those that cause respiratory infections, which can lead to chills.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold is rich in antioxidants like beta-carotene and lutein, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the severity of chill symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce fever and alleviate chill symptoms.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are rich in oil and vitamins that possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving body aches associated with chills.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the flowers using shade drying or oven drying at low temperature for 24 hours.
Grind 20g of dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 30g of capsules filler material such as potato starch in a bowl until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with chills because they contain the essential oils of Thymus serpyllum, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory herb. These oils have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the body, reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. As the capsules dissolve, the thyme oil is absorbed into the bloodstream, promoting relaxation and helping to warm the body from within. This can help to relieve chills and reduce discomfort, making it easier to recover from illness.
remedy_properties: Thymol: Thymol has antiviral and antibacterial properties, which helps to combat the underlying infection causing the chills by inhibiting the growth of pathogens.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in thyme have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms such as body aches and headaches associated with chills.

Cineole: Cineole, a monoterpene found in thyme, has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion caused by colds or flu.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, Thyme roots are used to create capsules that help combat bacterial and viral infections associated with chills.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like thymol, Thyme rhyzomes are a key component of medicinal capsules for chills as they exhibit anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at their peak potency and freshness.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or desiccation for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the thyme powder with gelatin capsules in a 1:5 ratio by weight and fill capsules immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When taken in capsule form, rosemary's essential oils are absorbed into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from chills caused by colds, sinus infections, or other viral illnesses. The herb's natural decongestant properties also help to clear congestion and sinus pressure, further alleviating chill-inducing symptoms. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties boost the immune system, helping to fight off underlying infections and reduce chill frequency.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help to combat infections and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to chills by fighting off pathogens that may be causing the chill.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, including rosmarinic acid, help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, which can alleviate symptoms of chills such as fever, headache, and body aches.

Vasodilatory properties: Rosemary's vasodilatory effects, due to the presence of camphor and borneol, help to dilate blood vessels, which can increase blood flow and warmth to the skin, potentially helping to alleviate cold or chilled sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing fever and alleviating symptoms associated with chills.
Barks: The barks of the rosemary plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and fever, making it an effective ingredient for medicinal capsules.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh rosemary leaves from your garden or local market.
Rinse the rosemary leaves thoroughly with filtered water and pat dry.
Air-dry the rosemary leaves for at least 24 hours in a single layer.
Combine 10g of dried rosmarinus officinalis powder with 100ml of glycerin and fill capsules.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with chills because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The silymarin compound found in milk thistle has been shown to help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness. Additionally, milk thistle's antioxidant properties help to boost the immune system, allowing it to better fight off infections that may be causing chills. By reducing inflammation and boosting immunity, milk thistle capsules provide a natural and effective way to combat chills and promote overall health and wellness.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Milk thistle's silymarin flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with chills.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid compounds in milk thistle have been shown to have antipyretic properties, meaning they can help to lower body temperature and relieve chills.
Phenolic acids: Milk thistle's phenolic acid content has been found to possess antimicrobial activity, which can help to combat infections that may be causing chills in the first place.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of milk thistle contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of chills.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat fever, chills, and other viral infections due to their high content of antioxidants and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and stems in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry silybum marianum plant material using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 8 hours.
Grind dried silybum marianum plant material into a fine powder using an herb grinder.
Mix 500mg of ground silyburn marianum with 100mg of silica gel in a small bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsules helps with chills because of its natural antipyretic properties, which effectively reduce body temperature. The herb contains a bioactive compound called thujone, which has been shown to possess potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that help combat the underlying causes of chills. By soothing the digestive system and promoting overall well-being, wormwood capsules can also alleviate symptoms such as fever, headache, and body ache associated with chills. This makes them a natural and effective solution for relieving chills and promoting recovery.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and camphor, help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause chills, reducing the risk of infection and alleviating symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to feelings of chilliness and discomfort.
Fever-reducing properties: Wormwood has been traditionally used to reduce fever, which is often a symptom of chills, by stimulating sweating and promoting the release of heat from the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils and sesquiterpenoids, which provide anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce chills.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, the barks have astringent and antipyretic effects, making them useful for reducing fever and body temperature.
remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and stems, drying them thoroughly in the sun or using a dehydrator for 2-3 hours.
Grind dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, measuring out 20-25 grams of powdered herb per serving.
Mix the powdered artemisia absinthium with 100-200mg of vegetable capsules, filling each capsule with approximately 1/4 teaspoon of the herbal mixture.
Place the filled capsules in an airtight container, storing them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.
Take two capsules twice daily, 30-60 minutes before meals, allowing the herbal properties to help alleviate chills and promote overall well-being.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chills that are treated with herbal capsules are fever, body ache, and flu. Echinacea-based capsules help boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Ginkgo biloba and ashwagandha supplements can help reduce fever and body aches caused by viral infections. Turmeric-containing capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that relieve joint pain and stiffness associated with chills. Additionally, ginger-based capsules can help soothe a sore throat and reduce fever.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with chills is a blend of Echinacea, Ginger, and Peppermint. Echinacea is known for boosting the immune system and reducing inflammation, while Ginger helps to warm the body and alleviate nausea. Peppermint, with its natural cooling properties, can help to reduce fever and soothe the digestive system. Taking these capsules together can help to alleviate chills, fever, and other symptoms associated with colds and flu. This combination can provide relief and promote overall well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), researchers investigated the efficacy of an echinacea compound herbal tea preparation (Echinacea Plus) in reducing cold and flu symptoms. The study involved 95 participants who were randomly assigned to receive either Echinacea Plus tea or a placebo, and the results showed that the Echinacea Plus group experienced a significant reduction in symptoms, lasting for a shorter period of time than the placebo group. This suggests that herbal capsules containing echinacea may be beneficial in alleviating chills associated with cold and flu symptoms.
